path.c(3) Library Functions Manual path.c(3)

NAME

path.c - Cross-platform path manipulation with normalization and Windows/Unix separator handling.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


void path_cleanup_thread_locals (void)
const char * extract_project_relative_path (const char *file)
char * expand_path (const char *path)
char * get_config_dir (void)
char * get_data_dir (void)
char * get_log_dir (void)
char * get_discovery_database_dir (void)
bool path_normalize_copy (const char *path, char *out, size_t out_len)
bool path_is_absolute (const char *path)
bool path_is_within_base (const char *path, const char *base)
bool path_is_within_any_base (const char *path, const char *const *bases, size_t base_count)
bool path_looks_like_path (const char *value)
asciichat_error_t path_validate_user_path (const char *input, path_role_t role, char **normalized_out)

Detailed Description

Cross-platform path manipulation with normalization and Windows/Unix separator handling.

Definition in file path.c.

Function Documentation

char * expand_path (const char * path)

Definition at line 484 of file path.c.

484                                     {
485   if (path[0] == PATH_TILDE) {
486     const char *home = platform_get_home_dir();
487     if (!home) {
488       return NULL;
489     }
490 
491     char *expanded;
492     size_t total_len = strlen(home) + strlen(path) + 1; // path includes the tilde
493     expanded = SAFE_MALLOC(total_len, char *);
494     if (!expanded) {
495       return NULL;
496     }
497     safe_snprintf(expanded, total_len, "%s%s", home, path + 1);
498 
499     platform_normalize_path_separators(expanded);
500 
501     return expanded;
502   }
503   return platform_strdup(path);
504 }




References platform_get_home_dir(),
    platform_normalize_path_separators(), platform_strdup(), and
    safe_snprintf().


Referenced by config_create_default(),
    config_load_and_apply(), detect_default_ssh_key(), and
    path_validate_user_path().






const
  char * extract_project_relative_path (const char * file)


Definition at line 435 of file path.c.


435                                                             {
436   if (!file) {
437     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "file is null");
438     return "unknown";
439   }
440 
441 #ifdef __EMSCRIPTEN__
442   // WASM builds: Return raw path to avoid recursion in logging system
443   // extract_project_relative_path is called from format_log_header, which is part of logging.
444   // If we call normalize_path or other functions that might use SET_ERRNO or log functions,
445   // we create infinite recursion: log_msg -> format_log_header -> extract_project_relative_path -> SET_ERRNO ->
446   // log_error -> log_msg For WASM, just return the filename without path processing.
447   const char *sep = strrchr(file, '/');
448   if (!sep) {
449     sep = strrchr(file, '\\');
450   }
451   return sep ? sep + 1 : file;
452 #endif
453 
454   /* First normalize the path to resolve .. and . components */
455   const char *normalized = normalize_path(file);
456 
457   /* Try to find and strip the project root from the absolute path */
458   char *project_root = find_project_root();
459   if (project_root) {
460     size_t root_len = strlen(project_root);
461     size_t norm_len = strlen(normalized);
462 
463     /* Check if normalized path starts with project root */
464     if (norm_len > root_len && strncmp(normalized, project_root, root_len) == 0) {
465       const char *remainder = normalized + root_len;
466 
467       /* Skip the separator if present */
468       if (*remainder == PATH_DELIM || *remainder == '/' || *remainder == '\\') {
469         remainder++;
470       }
471 
472       free(project_root);
473       return remainder;
474     }
475     free(project_root);
476   }
477 
478   /* Fallback: Project root detection failed, but we should still try to return
479    * a meaningful relative path, not just the filename. Return the normalized path
480    * which should contain the full directory structure. */
481   return normalized;
482 }






Referenced by asciichat_fatal_with_context(),
    asciichat_print_error_context(), log_json_async_safe(),
    log_json_write(), and log_template_apply().

  * get_config_dir (void )

  * get_data_dir (void )

511                          {
512   /* Delegate to platform abstraction layer */

References platform_get_data_dir().


Referenced by get_discovery_database_dir().






char
  * get_discovery_database_dir (void )


Definition at line 594 of file path.c.


594                                        {
595 #ifdef _WIN32
596   // Windows: Try %PROGRAMDATA%\ascii-chat\ first, then fall back to user directories
597   const char *program_data = platform_getenv("PROGRAMDATA");
598   if (program_data && program_data[0] != '\0') {
599     size_t len = strlen(program_data) + strlen("\\ascii-chat\\") + 1;
600     char *system_dir = SAFE_MALLOC(len, char *);
601     if (system_dir) {
602       safe_snprintf(system_dir, len, "%s\\ascii-chat\\", program_data);
603 
604       // Try to create directory recursively with public permissions (755 equivalent)
605       asciichat_error_t mkdir_result = platform_mkdir_recursive(system_dir, 0755);
606       if (mkdir_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
607         // Directory exists or was created - check if writable
608         if (platform_access(system_dir, PLATFORM_ACCESS_WRITE) == 0) {
609           return system_dir; // System-wide location is writable
610         }
611       }
612       SAFE_FREE(system_dir);
613     }
614   }
615 
616   // Fall back to user data directory
617   char *data_dir = get_data_dir();
618   if (data_dir) {
619     // Try to create the directory recursively
620     asciichat_error_t mkdir_result = platform_mkdir_recursive(data_dir, DIR_PERM_PRIVATE);
621     if (mkdir_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
622       if (platform_access(data_dir, PLATFORM_ACCESS_WRITE) == 0) {
623         return data_dir;
624       }
625     }
626     SAFE_FREE(data_dir);
627   }
628 
629   return NULL;
630 #else
631   // Unix: Try ${INSTALL_PREFIX}/var/ascii-chat/ first (system-wide, Homebrew-aware)
632   // This uses the baked-in install prefix from paths.h (e.g., /opt/homebrew or /usr/local)
633   const char *prefix = ASCIICHAT_INSTALL_PREFIX;
634   size_t system_len = strlen(prefix) + strlen("/var/ascii-chat/") + 1;
635   char *system_dir = SAFE_MALLOC(system_len, char *);
636   if (system_dir) {
637     safe_snprintf(system_dir, system_len, "%s/var/ascii-chat/", prefix);
638 
639     // Try to create directory recursively with public permissions (755) for system-wide use
640     // platform_mkdir_recursive creates parent directories as needed
641     asciichat_error_t mkdir_result = platform_mkdir_recursive(system_dir, 0755);
642     if (mkdir_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
643       // Directory exists or was created - check if writable
644       if (platform_access(system_dir, PLATFORM_ACCESS_WRITE) == 0) {
645         return system_dir; // System-wide location is writable
646       }
647     }
648     SAFE_FREE(system_dir);
649   }
650 
651 #ifdef __APPLE__
652   // On macOS, /usr/local is typically user-writable (set up by Homebrew)
653   // Try it if the install prefix is different (e.g., /opt/homebrew on Apple Silicon)
654   if (strcmp(prefix, "/usr/local") != 0) {
655     const char *usr_local_path = "/usr/local/var/ascii-chat/";
656     size_t usr_local_len = strlen(usr_local_path) + 1;
657     char *usr_local_dir = SAFE_MALLOC(usr_local_len, char *);
658     if (usr_local_dir) {
659       safe_snprintf(usr_local_dir, usr_local_len, "%s", usr_local_path);
660 
661       asciichat_error_t mkdir_result = platform_mkdir_recursive(usr_local_dir, 0755);
662       if (mkdir_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
663         if (platform_access(usr_local_dir, PLATFORM_ACCESS_WRITE) == 0) {
664           return usr_local_dir; // /usr/local is writable
665         }
666       }
667       SAFE_FREE(usr_local_dir);
668     }
669   }
670 #endif
671 
672   // Fall back to user data directory (XDG_DATA_HOME or ~/.local/share/ascii-chat/)
673   char *data_dir = get_data_dir();
674   if (data_dir) {
675     // Try to create the directory recursively
676     asciichat_error_t mkdir_result = platform_mkdir_recursive(data_dir, DIR_PERM_PRIVATE);
677     if (mkdir_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
678       if (platform_access(data_dir, PLATFORM_ACCESS_WRITE) == 0) {
679         return data_dir;
680       }
681     }
682     SAFE_FREE(data_dir);
683   }
684 
685   return NULL;
686 #endif
687 }






References get_data_dir(), platform_access(),
    platform_getenv(), platform_mkdir_recursive(), and
    safe_snprintf().


Referenced by options_init().






char
  * get_log_dir (void )


Definition at line 516 of file path.c.


516                         {
517 #ifdef __EMSCRIPTEN__
518   // WASM builds: Return NULL to skip SAFE_MALLOC before memory tracking is initialized
519   // The caller will use a fallback path (temp dir + filename)
520   return NULL;
521 #endif
522 
523 #ifdef NDEBUG
524   // Release builds: Use $TMPDIR/ascii-chat/
525   // Get system temp directory
526   char temp_dir[PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH];
527   if (!platform_get_temp_dir(temp_dir, sizeof(temp_dir))) {
528     // Fallback: Use current working directory if temp dir unavailable
529     char cwd_buf[PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH];
530     if (!platform_get_cwd(cwd_buf, sizeof(cwd_buf))) {
531       return NULL;
532     }
533     char *result = SAFE_MALLOC(strlen(cwd_buf) + 1, char *);
534     if (!result) {
535       return NULL;
536     }
537     safe_snprintf(result, strlen(cwd_buf) + 1, "%s", cwd_buf);
538     return result;
539   }
540 
541   // Build path to ascii-chat subdirectory
542   size_t log_dir_len = strlen(temp_dir) + strlen(PATH_SEPARATOR_STR) + strlen("ascii-chat") + 1;
543   char *log_dir = SAFE_MALLOC(log_dir_len, char *);
544   if (!log_dir) {
545     return NULL;
546   }
547   safe_snprintf(log_dir, log_dir_len, "%s%sascii-chat", temp_dir, PATH_SEPARATOR_STR);
548 
549   // Create the directory if it doesn't exist (with owner-only permissions)
550   asciichat_error_t mkdir_result = platform_mkdir(log_dir, DIR_PERM_PRIVATE);
551   if (mkdir_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
552     // Directory creation failed - fall back to temp_dir without subdirectory
553     SAFE_FREE(log_dir);
554     char *result = SAFE_MALLOC(strlen(temp_dir) + 1, char *);
555     if (!result) {
556       return NULL;
557     }
558     safe_snprintf(result, strlen(temp_dir) + 1, "%s", temp_dir);
559     return result;
560   }
561 
562   // Verify the directory is writable
563   if (platform_access(log_dir, PLATFORM_ACCESS_WRITE) != 0) {
564     // Directory not writable - fall back to temp_dir
565     SAFE_FREE(log_dir);
566     char *result = SAFE_MALLOC(strlen(temp_dir) + 1, char *);
567     if (!result) {
568       return NULL;
569     }
570     safe_snprintf(result, strlen(temp_dir) + 1, "%s", temp_dir);
571     return result;
572   }
573 
574   return log_dir;
575 #else
576   // Debug builds: Use repository root for logs
577   char *repo_root = find_project_root();
578   if (repo_root) {
579     return repo_root;
580   }
581 
582   // Fallback to current working directory if repo root not found
583   char cwd_buf[PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH];
584   if (!platform_get_cwd(cwd_buf, sizeof(cwd_buf))) {
585     return NULL;
586   }
587 
588   char *result = SAFE_MALLOC(strlen(cwd_buf) + 1, char *);
589   safe_snprintf(result, strlen(cwd_buf) + 1, "%s", cwd_buf);
590   return result;
591 #endif
592 }






References platform_access(), platform_get_cwd(),
    platform_get_temp_dir(), PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH, and
    safe_snprintf().






void
  path_cleanup_thread_locals (void )


Cleanup thread-local PCRE2 resources (now a no-op, kept for
    compatibility)




Previously this freed cached thread-local match data, but we now
    allocate fresh match data for each operation and free it immediately, so
    there's nothing to clean up. This function is kept for API
  compatibility.


Definition at line 83 of file path.c.


83                                       {
84   /* No-op: match data is now allocated and freed immediately per operation */
85 }






Referenced by main().






bool path_is_absolute (const char * path)


Definition at line 709 of file path.c.


709                                         {
710   if (!path || !*path) {
711     return false;
712   }
713 
714 #ifdef _WIN32
715   if ((path[0] == '\\' && path[1] == '\\')) {
716     return true; // UNC path
717   }
718   if (isalpha((unsigned char)path[0]) && path[1] == PATH_DRIVE_SEPARATOR && path[2] == PATH_DELIM) {
719     return true;
720   }
721   return false;
722 #else
723   return path[0] == PATH_DELIM;
724 #endif
725 }






Referenced by path_is_within_base(), and
    path_validate_user_path().






bool path_is_within_any_base (const char * path, const
  char *const * bases, size_t base_count)


Definition at line 761 of file path.c.


761                                                                                             {
762   if (!path || !bases || base_count == 0) {
763     return false;
764   }
765 
766   for (size_t i = 0; i < base_count; ++i) {
767     const char *base = bases[i];
768     if (!base) {
769       continue;
770     }
771     if (path_is_within_base(path, base)) {
772       return true;
773     }
774   }
775 
776   return false;
777 }

bool path_is_within_base (const char * path, const char *
  base)


Definition at line 727 of file path.c.


727                                                              {
728   if (!path || !base) {
729     return false;
730   }
731 
732   if (!path_is_absolute(path) || !path_is_absolute(base)) {
733     return false;
734   }
735 
736   char normalized_path[PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH];
737   char normalized_base[PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH];
738 
739   if (!path_normalize_copy(path, normalized_path, sizeof(normalized_path))) {
740     return false;
741   }
742   if (!path_normalize_copy(base, normalized_base, sizeof(normalized_base))) {
743     return false;
744   }
745 
746   size_t base_len = strlen(normalized_base);
747   if (base_len == 0) {
748     return false;
749   }
750 
751   if (platform_path_strcasecmp(normalized_path, normalized_base, base_len) != 0) {
752     return false;
753   }
754   char next = normalized_path[base_len];
755   if (next == '\0') {
756     return true;
757   }
758   return next == PATH_DELIM;
759 }

bool path_looks_like_path (const char * value)


Definition at line 779 of file path.c.


779                                              {
780   if (!value || *value == '\0') {
781     return false;
782   }
783 
784   if (value[0] == PATH_DELIM || value[0] == PATH_COMPONENT_DOT || value[0] == PATH_TILDE) {
785     return true;
786   }
787 
788   if (strchr(value, PATH_DELIM)) {
789     return true;
790   }
791 
792 #ifdef _WIN32

794     return true;
795   }
796 #endif
797 
798   return false;

bool path_normalize_copy (const char * path, char * out,
  size_t out_len)


Definition at line 689 of file path.c.


689                                                                       {
690   if (!path || !out || out_len == 0) {
691     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "null path or out or out_len is 0");
692     return false;
693   }
694 
695   const char *normalized = normalize_path(path);
696   if (!normalized) {
697     return false;
698   }
699 
700   size_t len = strlen(normalized);
701   if (len + 1 > out_len) {
702     return false;
703   }
704 
705   memcpy(out, normalized, len + 1);
706   return true;
707 }

    path_validate_user_path().






asciichat_error_t
  path_validate_user_path (const char * input, path_role_t role, char **
  normalized_out)


Definition at line 987 of file path.c.


987                                                                                                       {
988   if (!normalized_out) {
989     return SET_ERRNO(map_role_to_error(role), "path_validate_user_path requires output pointer");
990   }
991   *normalized_out = NULL;
992 
993   if (!input || *input == '\0') {
994     return SET_ERRNO(map_role_to_error(role), "Path is empty for role %d", role);
995   }
996 
997   // SECURITY: For log files, if input is a simple filename (no separators or ..), constrain it to a safe directory
998   if (role == PATH_ROLE_LOG_FILE) {
999     // Check if input contains path separators or parent directory references
1000     bool is_simple_filename = true;
1001     for (const char *p = input; *p; p++) {
1002       if (*p == PATH_DELIM || *p == '/' || *p == '\\') {
1003         is_simple_filename = false;
1004         break;
1005       }
1006     }
1007     // Also reject ".." components (even without separators like "..something")
1008     if (strstr(input, "..") != NULL) {
1009       is_simple_filename = false;
1010     }
1011 
1012     // If it's a simple filename, resolve it to a safe base directory
1013     if (is_simple_filename) {
1014       // Always prefer current working directory for simple log filenames
1015       // This ensures logs go to where the user is running the command from
1016       char safe_base[PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH];
1017 
1018       if (!platform_get_cwd(safe_base, sizeof(safe_base))) {
1019         // If cwd fails, try config dir as fallback
1020         char *config_dir = get_config_dir();
1021         if (config_dir) {
1022           SAFE_STRNCPY(safe_base, config_dir, sizeof(safe_base));
1023           SAFE_FREE(config_dir);
1024         } else {
1025           return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_LOGGING_INIT, "Failed to determine safe directory for log file");
1026         }
1027       }
1028 
1029       // Build the full path: safe_base + separator + input
1030       size_t base_len = strlen(safe_base);
1031       size_t input_len = strlen(input);
1032       bool needs_sep = base_len > 0 && safe_base[base_len - 1] != PATH_DELIM;
1033       size_t total_len = base_len + (needs_sep ? 1 : 0) + input_len + 1;
1034 
1035       if (total_len > PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH) {
1036         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_LOGGING_INIT, "Log file path too long: %s/%s", safe_base, input);
1037       }
1038 
1039       char resolved_buf[PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH];
1040       safe_snprintf(resolved_buf, sizeof(resolved_buf), "%s%s%s", safe_base, needs_sep ? PATH_SEPARATOR_STR : "",
1041                     input);
1042 
1043       // Normalize the resolved path
1044       char normalized_buf[PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH];
1045       if (!path_normalize_copy(resolved_buf, normalized_buf, sizeof(normalized_buf))) {
1046         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_LOGGING_INIT, "Failed to normalize log file path: %s", resolved_buf);
1047       }
1048 
1049       // Allocate and return the result
1050       char *result = SAFE_MALLOC(strlen(normalized_buf) + 1, char *);
1051       if (!result) {
1052         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate normalized path");
1053       }
1054       safe_snprintf(result, strlen(normalized_buf) + 1, "%s", normalized_buf);
1055       *normalized_out = result;
1056       return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1057     }
1058     // If not a simple filename (contains separators), continue with normal validation below
1059   }
1060 
1061   // For non-log-files or log files with path separators, validate as usual
1062   if (role != PATH_ROLE_LOG_FILE && !path_looks_like_path(input)) {
1063     return SET_ERRNO(map_role_to_error(role), "Value does not look like a filesystem path: %s", input);
1064   }
1065 
1066   char *expanded = expand_path(input);
1067   if (!expanded) {
1068     return SET_ERRNO(map_role_to_error(role), "Failed to expand path: %s", input);
1069   }
1070 
1071   char candidate_buf[PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH];
1072   const char *candidate_path = expanded;
1073 
1074   if (!path_is_absolute(candidate_path)) {
1075     char cwd_buf[PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH];
1076     if (!platform_get_cwd(cwd_buf, sizeof(cwd_buf))) {
1077       SAFE_FREE(expanded);
1078       return SET_ERRNO(map_role_to_error(role), "Failed to determine current working directory");
1079     }
1080 
1081     size_t total_len = strlen(cwd_buf) + 1 + strlen(candidate_path) + 1;
1082     if (total_len >= sizeof(candidate_buf)) {
1083       SAFE_FREE(expanded);
1084       return SET_ERRNO(map_role_to_error(role), "Resolved path is too long: %s/%s", cwd_buf, candidate_path);
1085     }
1086     if (strlen(candidate_path) > 0 && candidate_path[0] == PATH_DELIM) {
1087       safe_snprintf(candidate_buf, sizeof(candidate_buf), "%s%s", cwd_buf, candidate_path);
1088     } else {
1089       safe_snprintf(candidate_buf, sizeof(candidate_buf), "%s%c%s", cwd_buf, PATH_DELIM, candidate_path);
1090     }
1091     candidate_path = candidate_buf;
1092   }
1093 
1094   char normalized_buf[PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH];
1095   if (!path_normalize_copy(candidate_path, normalized_buf, sizeof(normalized_buf))) {
1096     SAFE_FREE(expanded);
1097     return SET_ERRNO(map_role_to_error(role), "Failed to normalize path: %s", candidate_path);
1098   }
1099 
1100   if (!path_is_absolute(normalized_buf)) {
1101     SAFE_FREE(expanded);
1102     return SET_ERRNO(map_role_to_error(role), "Normalized path is not absolute: %s", normalized_buf);
1103   }
1104 
1105   const char *bases[MAX_PATH_BASES] = {0};
1106   size_t base_count = 0;
1107 
1108   // Always add current working directory as an allowed base
1109   // This is critical for log files and other paths relative to where the user runs the command
1110   char cwd_base[PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH];
1111   if (platform_get_cwd(cwd_base, sizeof(cwd_base))) {
1112     append_base_if_valid(cwd_base, bases, &base_count);
1113   }
1114 
1115   char temp_base[PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH];
1116   if (platform_get_temp_dir(temp_base, sizeof(temp_base))) {
1117     append_base_if_valid(temp_base, bases, &base_count);
1118   }
1119 
1120   char *config_dir = get_config_dir();
1121   if (config_dir) {
1122     append_base_if_valid(config_dir, bases, &base_count);
1123   }
1124 
1125   const char *home_env = platform_get_home_dir();
1126   if (home_env) {
1127     append_base_if_valid(home_env, bases, &base_count);
1128   }
1129 
1130   char ascii_chat_home[PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH];
1131   if (home_env) {
1132     build_ascii_chat_path(home_env, ".ascii-chat", ascii_chat_home, sizeof(ascii_chat_home));
1133     append_base_if_valid(ascii_chat_home, bases, &base_count);
1134   }
1135 
1136 #ifndef _WIN32
1137   char ascii_chat_home_tmp[PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH];
1138   build_ascii_chat_path("/tmp", ".ascii-chat", ascii_chat_home_tmp, sizeof(ascii_chat_home_tmp));
1139   append_base_if_valid(ascii_chat_home_tmp, bases, &base_count);
1140 #endif
1141 
1142   char ssh_home[PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH];
1143   if (home_env) {
1144     build_ascii_chat_path(home_env, ".ssh", ssh_home, sizeof(ssh_home));
1145     append_base_if_valid(ssh_home, bases, &base_count);
1146   }
1147 
1148 #ifdef _WIN32
1149   char program_data_logs[PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH];
1150   const char *program_data = platform_getenv("PROGRAMDATA");
1151   if (program_data) {
1152     build_ascii_chat_path(program_data, "ascii-chat", program_data_logs, sizeof(program_data_logs));
1153     append_base_if_valid(program_data_logs, bases, &base_count);
1154   }
1155 #else
1156   // System-wide config directories (for server deployments)
1157   append_base_if_valid("/etc/ascii-chat", bases, &base_count);
1158   append_base_if_valid("/usr/local/etc/ascii-chat", bases, &base_count);
1159   append_base_if_valid("/var/log", bases, &base_count);
1160   append_base_if_valid("/var/tmp", bases, &base_count);
1161   append_base_if_valid("/tmp", bases, &base_count);
1162 #ifdef __APPLE__
1163   // On macOS, /tmp is a symlink to /private/tmp
1164   append_base_if_valid("/private/tmp", bases, &base_count);
1165   // On macOS, all user home directories are under /Users
1166   append_base_if_valid("/Users", bases, &base_count);
1167 #endif
1168 #endif
1169 
1170   // Security check: Reject paths that point to sensitive system files
1171   // This applies to all path roles, not just logs
1172   if (is_sensitive_system_path(normalized_buf)) {
1173     SAFE_FREE(expanded);
1174     if (config_dir) {
1175       SAFE_FREE(config_dir);
1176     }
1177     return SET_ERRNO(map_role_to_error(role), "Cannot write to protected system path: %s", normalized_buf);
1178   }
1179 
1180   // For log files, apply special validation rules
1181   if (role == PATH_ROLE_LOG_FILE) {
1182     // Check if path is a regular file (not a directory, not non-existent)
1183     // On macOS, fopen() can succeed on directories, so we must use platform_is_regular_file()
1184     bool is_regular_file = platform_is_regular_file(normalized_buf);
1185 
1186     // If a regular file exists, it MUST be an ascii-chat log or empty file to be overwritten
1187     if (is_regular_file && !is_existing_ascii_chat_log(normalized_buf) && !is_file_empty(normalized_buf)) {
1188       SAFE_FREE(expanded);
1189       if (config_dir) {
1190         SAFE_FREE(config_dir);
1191       }
1192       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_LOGGING_INIT,
1193                        "Cannot overwrite existing non-ascii-chat file: %s\n"
1194                        "For safety, ascii-chat will only overwrite its own log files or empty files",
1195                        normalized_buf);
1196     }
1197 
1198     // If file doesn't exist (or is a directory), check that path is in safe locations
1199     if (!is_regular_file) {
1200       bool allowed = base_count == 0 ? true : path_is_within_any_base(normalized_buf, bases, base_count);
1201       if (!allowed) {
1202         SAFE_FREE(expanded);
1203         if (config_dir) {
1204           SAFE_FREE(config_dir);
1205         }
1206         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_LOGGING_INIT,
1207                          "Log path %s is outside allowed directories (use -L /tmp/file.log, ~/file.log, or "
1208                          "relative/absolute paths in safe locations)",
1209                          normalized_buf);
1210       }
1211     }
1212   } else {
1213     // For non-log-file paths, apply standard whitelist validation
1214     bool allowed = base_count == 0 ? true : path_is_within_any_base(normalized_buf, bases, base_count);
1215     if (!allowed) {
1216       SAFE_FREE(expanded);
1217       if (config_dir) {
1218         SAFE_FREE(config_dir);
1219       }
1220       return SET_ERRNO(map_role_to_error(role), "Path %s is outside allowed directories", normalized_buf);
1221     }
1222   }
1223 
1224   char *result = SAFE_MALLOC(strlen(normalized_buf) + 1, char *);
1225   if (!result) {
1226     SAFE_FREE(expanded);
1227     if (config_dir) {
1228       SAFE_FREE(config_dir);
1229     }
1230     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate normalized path");
1231   }
1232   safe_snprintf(result, strlen(normalized_buf) + 1, "%s", normalized_buf);
1233   *normalized_out = result;
1234 
1235   SAFE_FREE(expanded);
1236   if (config_dir) {
1237     SAFE_FREE(config_dir);
1238   }
1239   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1240 }






References expand_path(), get_config_dir(),
    path_is_absolute(), path_is_within_any_base(),
    path_looks_like_path(), path_normalize_copy(),
    platform_get_cwd(), platform_get_home_dir(),
    platform_get_temp_dir(), platform_getenv(),
    platform_is_regular_file(), PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH,
    safe_snprintf(), and true.


Referenced by config_create_default(),
    config_load_and_apply(), parse_keys_from_file(),
    parse_log_file(), parse_private_key(),
    parse_public_key(), and validate_ssh_key_file().








Author


Generated automatically by Doxygen for ascii-chat from the source
    code.







  

    Version 0.9.0
    ascii-chat
